content: {"perspective": "Industry", "question": "What do real-world usage, retention, and adherence data from commercial intermittent-fasting tracking apps (Zero, Fastic, Simple, LIFE Fasting Tracker, DoFasting, Window, Lasta) tell us about how the RCT literature generalizes to unsupervised self-paying consumers — specifically: day-30 and day-90 retention, drop-off curves by protocol (16:8 TRE vs 5:2 vs ADF), user-reported weight-loss outcomes, subscription churn signals after the AHA 2024 cardiovascular mortality news cycle, and any publicly disclosed app user-base outcome data from company press releases, investor decks, or academic partnerships?", "search_queries": ["Zero intermittent fasting app user retention adherence 30 day 90 day real-world outcomes", "commercial fasting tracking app Simple Fastic LIFE user base weight loss outcomes real-world data", "intermittent fasting app subscription churn 2024 cardiovascular death news AHA consumer wellness", "digital health fasting app academic partnership published real-world evidence user outcomes", "Zero fasting app Big Sky Health investor deck user metrics market share retention MAU"]}
